title: How energy statements work
subtitle: The release-and-allow method from Rebecca's The Money Zone.
excerpt: A reference article for the energy-statement practices in the library. Explains the Release / Allow structure from The Money Zone, why each statement is said three times, and how to use the statements alongside tapping or rituals.
sourceNotes: Drawn from The Money Zone: How To Release Hidden Resistance, Allow Flow And Manifest Millions (Rebecca Page, 2024), chapters 2 through 5. The Release / Allow structure is Rebecca's own framework.

practice.whenToUse: Read once before starting any energy-statement practice. Re-read when a statement isn't landing.

glossaryTerms.definition: The core movement of Rebecca's Money Zone method. Two statements: one releasing the resistance, one allowing what's wanted. Each said three times.
Rebecca's energy-alignment method set out in The Money Zone (2024). Five steps: Question, Check, Release, Confirm, Allow. The Release / Allow statements are the practice surface.

content.text: Energy statements are short spoken practices drawn from Rebecca's Money Zone method, set out in her 2024 book of the same name. The shape is simple: two statements, said three times each. One releases the resistance the user can feel; one allows what the user wants. The pair is enough to work most situations the practice is for — money worry, self-worth, the guilt of wanting more.
"I am ready to release [name what you are releasing]. I release it now. I release it now. I release it now." Said three times in total, slowly.
"I am ready to align with and allow [name what you are allowing]. I allow it now. I allow it now. I allow it now." Said three times in total, slowly.
Why each is said three times

The triple repetition is the structure of the practice. Rebecca uses three across all her work — three set-up statements in tapping, three repetitions of release and allow, three rounds of a tapping script. Once is too quick to register; twice can feel like a stumble; three lands. Use three.

Statements are first-person, present tense, positive construction. "I allow it now" not "I will allow it" (future tense pushes the new reality away). "I am safe with money" not "I am not afraid of money" (negation reinforces the noun being negated).
Where the truth of a present-tense statement feels far away, name a smaller truer version. "I am safe with money" may not land yet; "I am safe right now" usually does. Build up from what is true.

The statements in the library are templates. Change the wording where it fits the situation better. The structure (release first, allow second, three times each, present tense, positive construction) is what holds.

Energy statements work on their own and they pair well with tapping. The pattern Rebecca uses in The Money Zone is to find the resistance using a body cue (her "Sway", see the book), do the Release statement, check that the resistance has cleared, then do the Allow. A tapping round in the middle is optional but often useful for stronger resistance: tap the named feeling first, then say the statements.

The Release / Allow structure is set out in chapters 2 through 5 of The Money Zone: How To Release Hidden Resistance, Allow Flow And Manifest Millions (Rebecca Page, 2024). The wider Zone Method has five steps; the two statements are the spoken practice surface. Rebecca's earlier exposure to similar techniques (Yvette Taylor's Energy Alignment Method, kinesiology muscle-testing) is documented in chapter 1 of the book; the Release / Allow wording in this library is hers.
